vehicle.label: Tiltrotor VTOL Hybrid
vehicle.desc: Fixed-wing VTOL with 2 tilting rotor nacelles, BVLOS long-range capable

parts.label: Center Fuselage Spine
parts.spec: CFRP box beam center fuselage, avionics bay, wing spar socket, front GPS boom mount, belly camera window

parts.label: Main Wing (2-piece)
parts.spec: Straight wing, 4S NACA 2412, dual 15mm CFRP spar, 900mm half-span, 160mm chord, 3-layer CFRP skin

parts.label: Nacelle Assembly x 2
parts.spec: Tilting nacelle assembly, 0-90deg tilt axis, 6309 bearing set, servo-driven worm gear, motor and ESC pocket

parts.label: Tilt Servo + Linkage x 2
parts.spec: 25kg-cm DS3218 servo per nacelle, titanium pushrod, dual ball joints, tilt range 0-93deg, crash-tolerant

parts.label: V-Tail x 2 (Ruddervator)
parts.spec: +-35deg V-tail ruddervator pair, MG996R servo each, 30deg dihedral, CFRP sandwich panel, hinge piano wire

parts.label: Landing Gear (Tri)
parts.spec: Tricycle landing gear, spring-steel skid arms, 55mm dia wheels, CG at 1/3 chord, 200mm wheelbase

parts.label: Battery Bay (Slide-In)
parts.spec: Belly slide-in battery bay, 6S 8000mAh Li-ion pack, Velcro + strap dual retention, CG position 28% MAC

parts.label: Payload Nose Bay
parts.spec: Interchangeable nose payload bay, Day/IR camera gimbal or survey sensor, 80x60x50mm, 300g payload limit

parts.label: Tilt-Axis Harness + Datum Service Bay
parts.spec: Tiltrotor integration_service module with tilt-axis harness_channel, slack_loop, interface_datum, service_hatch, connector keying, and nacelle-to-wing load-path inspection access

risk.desc: Potential misalignment during assembly due to complex interface geometries.
risk.mit: Implement detailed assembly instructions and training for operators.

risk.desc: Risk of thermal stress on avionics bay due to insufficient insulation.
risk.mit: Increase insulation thickness or add thermal barriers.

risk.desc: Camera window may leak if seal is compromised during assembly.
risk.mit: Use robust sealing materials and conduct thorough pressure tests.

risk.desc: Insufficient clearance around moving parts could lead to jamming.
risk.mit: Review and adjust clearances for all moving interfaces.

risk.desc: Inadequate support structures during manufacturing may cause defects.
risk.mit: Optimize support orientation and ensure minimal interference.

_subsystem_plan.subsystem_role: Structural spine and avionics bay housing

physics_paths.path: Wing spar load -> Fuselage spine -> Landing gear mount
physics_paths.description: Carries wing spar loads into fuselage spine and distributes to landing gear mount.

physics_paths.path: Avionics cooling air -> Avionics bay -> Exhaust vents
physics_paths.description: Cools avionics components and exhausts hot air.

physics_paths.path: Solar heat -> Outer shell -> Inner insulation -> Avionics bay
physics_paths.description: Manages thermal loads on avionics bay.

physics_paths.path: Nacelle tilt axis -> Bearing seat -> Fuselage spine
physics_paths.description: Supports nacelle tilt mechanism and ensures smooth rotation.

physics_paths.path: Belly camera window -> Camera lens -> External environment
physics_paths.description: Maintains pressure integrity around belly camera window.

physics_paths.path: Avionics bay wiring harness -> Harness passthrough -> Flight control computer
physics_paths.description: Connects avionics bay wiring to flight control computer.

physics_paths.path: Service hatch -> Avionics bay -> Maintenance access
physics_paths.description: Provides access for maintenance and servicing of avionics bay.

integration_opportunities.opportunity: Add additional service hatches for easier maintenance access.
integration_opportunities.benefit: Improves accessibility for routine maintenance tasks.

integration_opportunities.opportunity: Integrate structural reinforcements for increased durability.
integration_opportunities.benefit: Enhances overall structural integrity and longevity.

integration_opportunities.opportunity: Optimize aerodynamic shape for reduced drag.

manufacturing_strategy.datum: Primary locating faces and axis rings
manufacturing_strategy.support_orientation: Support structures oriented for minimal interference with final part geometry
manufacturing_strategy.inspection: Visual inspection for surface finish and dimensional accuracy, non-destructive testing for structural integrity

verification_focus.focus: Load-bearing capacity of fuselage spine and spar socket
Fit and function of bolted and snap-fit interfaces

requirements: Straight wing, 4S NACA 2412 airfoil, dual 15mm CFRP spar, 900mm half-span, 160mm chord, 3-layer CFRP skin
Fixed-wing VTOL with 2 tilting rotor nacelles, BVLOS long-range capable
Subassembly must carry nacelle loads into fuselage spine; attach V-tail/ruddervator and landing gear
Interfaces must be visible and labeled, with clearances stated
Hardware features must match real catalog sizes
Tilt axis, bearing seats, servo/linkage access, and nacelle rotation clearance must be visible
Wing spar socket, fuselage spine, and payload/battery service paths must be separate and inspectable
Design must remain educational mockup, avoiding operational flight tuning or control-law details
Nacelle loads must route through bearing seats into wing structure via named fittings, with motor/servo modules remaining replaceable
Critical joints must have visible insert/washer/locking/preload evidence and torque/preload intent
